county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Yoakum County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Plains. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Plains. South Plains county organized 1907. Yoakum County Historical Commission. Farming and oil. Check Bexar for pre-organization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Yoakum County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Plains holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Yoakum County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1876 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Yoakum County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1876. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Yoakum County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1876. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Yoakum County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1876 to 1950 for Yoakum County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Yoakum County Oil and Gas Lease Records -- GLO / County Clerk
source_profiles.repository: Texas General Land Office, Austin / County Clerk, Plains
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease records, mineral rights transfers, and related land documents from 1920. Texas GLO maintains state mineral records. County Clerk has private mineral lease records.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Yoakum County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Yoakum County from 1876. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Young County formed 1856 from Bosque, Fannin. County seat is Graham. North Texas frontier region with ranching and Indian defense heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Graham. North-central Texas county organized 1874. Fort Belknap (major frontier military post) — records at state historic site and NARA. Young County Historical Commission. Butterfield Overland Mail route heritage. Check Bosque and Fannin for pre-organization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Young County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1856; County Clerk in Graham holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Young County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1856 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Young County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1856. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Young County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1856. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Young County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1856 to 1950 for Young County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Young County Area (Comanche and Kiowa)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for Comanche and Kiowa in the Young County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.source_name: Young County Oil and Gas Lease Records -- GLO / County Clerk
source_profiles.repository: Texas General Land Office, Austin / County Clerk, Graham
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease records, mineral rights transfers, and related land documents from 1917. Texas GLO maintains state mineral records. County Clerk has private mineral lease records.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Young County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Young County from 1856. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Zapata County formed 1858 from Starr, Webb. County seat is Zapata. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Zapata. Rio Grande Valley border county. Zapata County Museum of History. Falcon Reservoir (Old Zapata now submerged). Significant Mexican-American heritage. Mexican land grant records. Check Starr and Webb for pre-1858 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Zapata County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in Zapata holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Zapata County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1858 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Zapata County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1858. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Zapata County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1858. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Zapata County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1858 to 1950 for Zapata County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Spanish Colonial / Mission Records -- Zapata County Area
source_profiles.repository: Catholic Archives of San Antonio / TSLAC / Bexar Archives at UT-Austin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Spanish colonial records including mission registers (baptisms, marriages, burials), presidio records, and land grants. Zapata County area was part of the Spanish colonial system. Records at Catholic Archives of San Antonio, TSLAC, and Bexar Archives at UT-Austin.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Colonial-era records vary in survival; many originals at Catholic Archives or in Mexican archives

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Zapata County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Zapata County from 1858. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Zavala County formed 1858 from Maverick, Uvalde. County seat is Crystal City. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Crystal City. South Texas county organized 1884. Crystal City was site of WWII internment camp (German, Japanese, Italian POWs and internees) — records at NARA. Zavala County Historical Commission. Check Maverick and Uvalde for pre-organization records. Strong Mexican-American heritage. Spinach capital heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Zavala County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in Crystal City holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Zavala County from 1858. GLO archives include searchable online databases.
